topic: Artistic Culture in the French-speaking World
subject: french

items.q: What does the 'Nouvelle Vague' refer to?
items.correct: A French cinema movement of the late 1950s–60s
items.random_wrong: A type of wave at the beach
items.why: Students take 'Nouvelle Vague' literally; it is a landmark film movement (Truffaut, Godard), key to francophone artistic culture.

items.q: Is francophone literature limited to writers from France?
items.correct: No — it includes major authors from Africa, the Caribbean and Quebec
items.random_wrong: Only writers who write in English
items.why: Students wrongly equate 'francophone' with France alone; the artistic culture spans the whole French-speaking world.

items.q: What is celebrated at the Fête de la Musique on 21 June?
items.correct: Music, with free concerts across France
items.why: Students confuse French cultural festivals; the Fête de la Musique celebrates music, while Cannes celebrates film.

items.q: Which annual film festival held on the French Riviera is a flagship event of francophone cinema?
items.why: It's Le Festival de Cannes. “La Fête de la Musique” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: The 'Nouvelle Vague' refers to a movement in which French art form?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Cinema. “Painting” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: La Fête de la Musique, held every 21 June, celebrates which art form across France?
items.why: It's Music. “Cinema” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which statement about francophone literature is correct?
items.correct: It includes major authors from Africa, the Caribbean and Quebec, not only France
items.misconception: It is written only by authors born in mainland France
items.random_wrong: It must always be translated from English
items.why: Correct answer: It includes major authors from Africa, the Caribbean and Quebec, not only France. “It is written only by authors born in mainland France” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
